tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: Escalation continues.

The US and Iran continue to bomb each other, but no tankers have been attacked, at least not any that have been publicly reported.

Mainstream media in the past 48 hours really started to pick up on the notion that the oil shuttling service through the Oman lane is slowing down. Based on satellite data, it is not completely dead yet, but how much are the shipowners willing to risk it? 

Well, they are having a hard time getting crew members to agree, and a few have already reported that the captains have refused transit.

In the meantime, the Iran lane is getting all the traffic with AIS turned on. No visible tanker transits through the Oman lane, as expected.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: Escalation continues.

The US and Iran continue to bomb each other, but no tankers have been attacked, at least not any that have been publicly reported.

Mainstream media in the past 48 hours really started to pick up on the notion that the oil shuttling service through the Oman lane is slowing down. Based on satellite data, it is not completely dead yet, but how much are the shipowners willing to risk it? 

Well, they are having a hard time getting crew members to agree, and a few have already reported that the captains have refused transit.

In the meantime, the Iran lane is getting all the traffic with AIS turned on. No visible tanker transits through the Oman lane, as expected.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

tweets.text:
The newly unredacted emails raise the serious possibility that the ATO pushed for the shutdown and global press conference to create an official event that would validate Nick McKenzie’s reporting and help him defend against my defamation lawsuit. Julie Holt specifically cited McKenzie’s interest when proposing the global event and drafted much of the messaging Jim Lee read during the press conference. McKenzie then used the shutdown as evidence in his defense. I do not yet have the specific communications proving the full arrangement, but the circumstantial evidence is compelling.

What’s very frustrating is that it’s likely that, had I not filed my successful defamation lawsuit, my bank never would have been shut down and none of its customers would have lost a penny.
tweets.full_text:
The newly unredacted emails raise the serious possibility that the ATO pushed for the shutdown and global press conference to create an official event that would validate Nick McKenzie’s reporting and help him defend against my defamation lawsuit. Julie Holt specifically cited McKenzie’s interest when proposing the global event and drafted much of the messaging Jim Lee read during the press conference. McKenzie then used the shutdown as evidence in his defense. I do not yet have the specific communications proving the full arrangement, but the circumstantial evidence is compelling.

What’s very frustrating is that it’s likely that, had I not filed my successful defamation lawsuit, my bank never would have been shut down and none of its customers would have lost a penny.

tweets.text: I now know that the remarks read by IRS-CI Chief Jim Lee during the press conference announcing the shutdown of my bank were written by Julie Holt, Director of Crime and Fraud Communications at the Australian Taxation Office. Based on her emails, which specifically mention Nick McKenzie on multiple occasions, I believe she was acting on McKenzie’s behalf. The judge had not yet ruled on my defamation lawsuit, and the action against my bank was designed to be used by McKenzie in defending against my lawsuit. McKenzie did, in fact, introduce the action against my bank as part of his defense, but he lost the lawsuit anyway. Now I am hoping I can file another lawsuit based on this new evidence. @Ageinvestigates
tweets.full_text: I now know that the remarks read by IRS-CI Chief Jim Lee during the press conference announcing the shutdown of my bank were written by Julie Holt, Director of Crime and Fraud Communications at the Australian Taxation Office. Based on her emails, which specifically mention Nick McKenzie on multiple occasions, I believe she was acting on McKenzie’s behalf. The judge had not yet ruled on my defamation lawsuit, and the action against my bank was designed to be used by McKenzie in defending against my lawsuit. McKenzie did, in fact, introduce the action against my bank as part of his defense, but he lost the lawsuit anyway. Now I am hoping I can file another lawsuit based on this new evidence. @Ageinvestigates

tweets.text: Based on newly unredacted IRS emails, it’s clear that the Australian Taxation Office was the driving force behind the IRS effort to get OCIF to shut down my bank as a J5 publicity stunt. It was likely also a favor for @Ageinvestigates, who I successfully sued for defamation.
tweets.full_text: Based on newly unredacted IRS emails, it’s clear that the Australian Taxation Office was the driving force behind the IRS effort to get OCIF to shut down my bank as a J5 publicity stunt. It was likely also a favor for @Ageinvestigates, who I successfully sued for defamation.

tweets.text: The OCIF Commissioner told the IRS she would deliberately delay serving the C&D order until shortly before the press conference to prevent the bank from obtaining an injunction before the closure was implemented and publicly announced. This was highly unethical & likely illegal.
tweets.full_text: The OCIF Commissioner told the IRS she would deliberately delay serving the C&D order until shortly before the press conference to prevent the bank from obtaining an injunction before the closure was implemented and publicly announced. This was highly unethical & likely illegal.

tweets.text: I just read the J5’s detailed 6-page communications plan, likely written by the ATO, to use the media to turn its failed Atlantis investigation into a fake success and frame my bank for tax evasion and money laundering for the sole purpose of improving the J5’s global reputation.
tweets.full_text: I just read the J5’s detailed 6-page communications plan, likely written by the ATO, to use the media to turn its failed Atlantis investigation into a fake success and frame my bank for tax evasion and money laundering for the sole purpose of improving the J5’s global reputation.

tweets.text:
I just read another unredacted email showing that former IRS-CI Chief Jim Lee, on the morning he spoke at the press conference announcing the closure of my bank, still had no idea what the bank had actually done to facilitate tax evasion or money laundering. So, until the day of the press conference, he had no idea that the bank was completely innocent and that the entire press conference was a publicity stunt.

He asked Justin Cole, the IRS-CI communications director, for two brief examples of what the bank had done to help its customers evade taxes or launder money. The answer is redacted, but I know for a fact that Cole could not come up with even one example because he knew the bank had done nothing wrong.
 
That’s why Lee did not mention a single example of anything the bank did to help its customers launder money or evade taxes, and that’s why the OCIF Commissioner stated that her investigation found no evidence that the bank helped its customers launder money or evade taxes.
https://t.co/oX061yNqLB

So, until the day of the press conference, he had no idea that the bank was completely innocent and that the entire press conference was a publicity stunt.

He asked Justin Cole, the IRS-CI communications director, for two brief examples of what the bank had done to help its customers evade taxes or launder money. The answer is redacted, but I know for a fact that Cole could not come up with even one example because he knew the bank had done nothing wrong.
 
That’s why Lee did not mention a single example of anything the bank did to help its customers launder money or evade taxes, and that’s why the OCIF Commissioner stated that her investigation found no evidence that the bank helped its customers launder money or evade taxes.
https://t.co/oX061yNqLB

I honestly don’t think there is a bigger scam than trading card hobby boxes.  The RTP is literally 1%, meaning 99% loss is the most common outcome.

Complete joke of an industry.  $500-1k boxes that yield all trash.  

I say this every time I rip them, but this time I’m serious - never again.
